description: A contribution record in The Ptah Protocol. The permission and attribution layer — governs how someone who did not create a world adds something to it, and ensures that addition is attributed, traceable, and governed by whatever rules the world originator set.

main.description: A deed of addition. Imhotep — the most famous architect in history, deified as the Son of Ptah. Every contributor to a world is an Imhotep. They build within the tradition of the master. Their work is attributed. The lineage is unbroken.

worldReference.description: The AT URI of the world being contributed to.

contributionType.description: What kind of contribution and which record type it links to.

recordReference.description: The AT URI of the primary record being contributed. The Contribution record is the anchor of attribution — not the exhaustive list of every record the contributor touched. The full scope is discoverable via DID query across record types.

originatorApproval.description: The approval status of this contribution.

canonicalStatus.description: The canonical standing of this contribution within the world.

attributionChain.description: An array tracing every hand this contribution passed through. AT URIs of prior contribution records or DIDs. This is the Carryforward logic expressed at the protocol layer.

publicDomainCompliance.description: Whether this contribution complies with public domain sourcing requirements. Relevant for catalog worlds.
